title: uragoga fraterna
intro: Uragoga fraterna, also known as the bitter berry tree, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, characterized by its dark green leaves and small, yellowish-green flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for treating various health conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Uragoga fraterna is also believed to have health benefits such as improving digestion, reducing inflammation, and supporting the immune system. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its bark and roots are used to make herbal teas and tonics.
